text:
To find out how many miles farther Shandy drove compared to Matteo, we first need to calculate the distance each of them traveled using the formula:

\[ \text{Distance} = \text{Speed} \times \text{Time} \]

For Matteo:
- Speed: 55 miles per hour
- Time: 4 hours

\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} = 55 \, \text{miles/hour} \times 4 \, \text{hours} = 220 \, \text{miles} \]

For Shandy:
- Speed: 45 miles per hour
- Time: 10 hours

\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} = 45 \, \text{miles/hour} \times 10 \, \text{hours} = 450 \, \text{miles} \]

Now we find the difference in the distances they traveled:

\[ \text{Difference} = \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} - \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} = 450 \, \text{miles} - 220 \, \text{miles} = 230 \, \text{miles} \]

So, Shandy drove 230 miles farther than Matteo.
